data.body:
## Description

Create tests/hooks/test-fix-bug-skill-directive.sh — a behavioral test that pipes JSON input to the hook script and asserts on stdout content and exit code.

### Test cases
1. Pipe message containing '/fix-bug' → assert stdout contains 'Skill' directive text
2. Pipe message containing '/dso:fix-bug' → assert same directive output
3. Pipe message NOT containing /fix-bug (e.g., 'fix the login bug') → assert empty stdout
4. Pipe message with '/fix-bug' embedded in longer sentence → assert directive fires
5. All cases: assert exit code 0 (hooks must never fail)
6. Pipe message containing 'fix-bug' WITHOUT leading slash (e.g., 'the fix-bug process') → assert empty stdout (slash-anchored matching, no false positives on bare substring)

### Test approach
Each test creates a JSON stdin payload, pipes it to the hook script, and asserts on stdout content and exit code. Uses the standard test function pattern from this repo (test_NAME functions).

### Field name note
The actual UserPromptSubmit JSON payload field name from Claude Code is unknown. Tests should use a configurable field name via env var (e.g., HOOK_FIELD_NAME=user_message) so tests can be updated once the real field name is discovered. Default to 'user_message' as the initial assumption.

### TDD
This IS the RED test task — tests fail until Task 2 creates the script.

Add .test-index RED marker: plugins/dso/hooks/fix-bug-skill-directive.sh: tests/hooks/test-fix-bug-skill-directive.sh [test_fix_bug_pattern_match]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0) — RED markers tolerate expected failures
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] Test file exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/hooks/test-fix-bug-skill-directive.sh
- [ ] Test file contains at least 5 test functions
  Verify: grep -c '^test_' tests/hooks/test-fix-bug-skill-directive.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 5)}'
- [ ] .test-index RED marker added
  Verify: grep -q 'fix-bug-skill-directive' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
